body:
# Minimal Node.js HTTP server returning "Hello, World!"

## Context

We need a minimal HTTP server implemented with the Node.js built-in `http`
module only â€” **no external web framework** (no Express, Fastify, Koa, etc.)
and no third-party runtime dependencies. The server is written as ESM
(`import` syntax; `"type": "module"` in `package.json`).

Behavior:

- Listens on the port given by the `PORT` environment variable, defaulting to
  `3000` when `PORT` is unset or empty.
- A `GET` request to path `/` returns HTTP `200` with a `Content-Type` header
  of `text/html` and a response body containing the exact text
  `Hello, World!`.
- An npm `start` script launches the server.

The whole implementation stays to **a single source file plus
`package.json`** â€” no build step, no bundler, no config sprawl.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `package.json` exists with `"type": "module"` and a `"start"` script
      that launches the server (e.g. `node server.js`), and declares **no**
      runtime dependencies (a web framework is explicitly disallowed).
- [ ] A single ESM source file creates an HTTP server using only the Node.js
      built-in `http` (or `node:http`) module.
- [ ] The server binds to `process.env.PORT` when set, and defaults to `3000`
      when `PORT` is unset or empty.
- [ ] A `GET /` request responds with status `200`, a `Content-Type` header
      equal to `text/html`, and a body whose text contains exactly
      `Hello, World!`.
- [ ] `npm start` (or `PORT=<n> npm start`) boots the server and the running
      server can be verified end-to-end with `curl -i http://localhost:<port>/`
      showing `200`, `Content-Type: text/html`, and the `Hello, World!` body.

## Out of Scope

- Any external web framework or third-party runtime dependency.
- Routing beyond `GET /` (other paths/methods may return a simple default such
  as `404`, but no router abstraction is introduced).
- HTTPS/TLS, HTTP/2, clustering, graceful-shutdown handling, or process
  managers.
- Logging frameworks, environment-file loaders (`dotenv`), linters, or a
  test-runner setup beyond what is needed to verify the acceptance criteria.
- Containerization / deployment manifests.

## Notes

- Keep `Content-Type` exactly `text/html` (the acceptance check compares the
  media type; adding `; charset=utf-8` would change the header value the
  criterion pins â€” match `text/html` unless the driver tolerates a charset
  suffix).
- Read the port as `process.env.PORT || 3000` (or `Number(process.env.PORT) ||
  3000`) so an unset/empty value falls back to `3000`.
- Suggested file name: `server.js`, referenced by the `start` script as
  `node server.js`.
- No `Epic: #N` reference â€” this is a standalone Story.
title: Minimal Node.js HTTP server returning "Hello, World!"
